text.text: A small but perfectly formed exhibition of Egyptian artefacts. Beautiful curation, well organised ticketing and a generally respectful environment within the space. The tickets are a bit steep considering they don't include access to the permanent exhibition. However, this museum has picturesque grounds, good facilities, is clean and well-maintained. Worth a trip and a good half-day outing with picnic options. Easily walkable from the station and there's a Youbike station in the parking lot so you can explore the grounds.

text.text:
Taking a train to Bao'an station then walk about 15 min to the museum would be an option if you are not driving. The architecture and the compounds are beautiful. Considering only two levels, the collection is quite impressive and they are all clearly categorised, with sufficient information about each artefact or exhibit. There will be a new Egypt exhibit starting in Jan next year which will last for nearly a year.

I saw quite a number of people having picnic at the park near the museum or just simply lying on the grass patch to relax. There is also a 7-11, Starbucks and a few stalls selling sausage and ice cream if you feel peckish.

text.text:
A very well designed/decorated hotel lacking the simple things. Love the design, the addition of neuvo items to add to the ambience however the rooms are not for families. If you have kids and value any privacy at all in the bathroom then this isn’t the hotel for you…or if you want to sit down on an actual chair in your hotel room as there aren’t any other than a half full bean bag chair.

The breakfast offered although tasty is a lunch dinner menu for breakfast so except to eat a burger at 8am not toast or anything to do with breakfast at all.

The good though is really good. It is a beautiful hotel and if I was 20 years younger it would have been cool to stay here. With 2 kids in town and luggage for a family it was however not fun as there just isn’t any room to open luggage in the room and actually walk around.

The staff. They are by far some of the best hotel staff that I have seen. Attention to detail, helpful and super friendly and if their boss reads this they all deserve a raise.

text.text:
I recently stayed at this hotel and found it to be more of a stylish hostel than a traditional hotel. While it has some unique design elements, such as exposed brick walls, it may not meet the expectations of those looking for a full-service hotel experience.

The rooms are on the smaller side, and while the service is decent, it isn’t at the level of a five-star hotel. There are some thoughtful touches, like a well-equipped common area and good location near eateries, but certain aspects—such as the lack of in-room heating—may be a drawback, especially in colder weather.

Overall, it’s a reasonable choice for those who enjoy boutique-style accommodations, but for the same price, I would personally opt for a five-star hotel with more amenities and a proper buffet breakfast.

text.text: Clean, small and cozy place, nice staff. Diverse cake menu, updated every day on IG @chaoticbox_xiaoman, you can follow and pre-order if you are afraid of running out of stock.  All cakes are very delicious and have a very distinctive taste with fruit sauce. Highly recommend.

text.text:
The interior space isn't large, but the atmosphere is superb, both refined and comfortable.

For food, we ordered the "Shizuoka Hojicha Mille Crepe" and the "Candied Peach Oolong Tea Mille Crepe." The mille crepe cakes were incredibly layered and delicately textured, with a rich and satisfying hojicha flavor. The peach oolong tea offered a subtle blend of fruity and tea aromas, and every bite showcased the high-quality ingredients.

For drinks, we ordered the "White Grape Oolong Tea" and the "Strawberry Sparkling Tea." Both were perfectly balanced and delicious, especially the refreshing sparkling tea, which complemented the cakes flawlessly. The afternoon tea wasn't overly sweet at all; instead, it was incredibly refreshing.

Overall, from the ambiance to the quality of the desserts and drinks, the owner's dedication is evident. It's a highly recommended dessert shop in Tainan.

originalText.text:
內用空間不算大，但店內營造出的氛圍感極佳，整體感覺既精緻又舒適。

餐點部分點了「靜岡焙茶千層」與「糖漬桃子烏龍茶千層」，千層蛋糕的層次非常豐富且口感細膩，焙茶味濃厚到位，桃子烏龍則帶有淡淡果香與茶香的層次感，每一口都能感受到食材的真材實料。

飲品點了「白葡萄烏龍茶」與「草莓雷夢氣泡」，味道調配得非常順口好喝，特別是氣泡飲的清爽感與蛋糕搭配得天衣無縫，整份下午茶吃下來完全不會感到甜膩，反而覺得非常爽口。

整體而言，不管是空間環境的營造還是甜點與飲品的水準，都讓人感受到店家的用心，是來到台南非常推薦的質感甜點店。

text.text:
We visited this bar on our second night in Tainan and were met with an unexpected sense of ease. The owner-bartender welcomed us in English the moment we stepped in, even remembering the note about our anniversary. He checked in just enough, sharing small parts of the bar’s story - the way each element comes together through collaborations with designers, photographers, and roasters.

The space itself was warm and modern, the kind of room that feels thoughtfully put together without trying too hard. At some point, I realised I felt unusually at home for a place I had never been to. Maybe it was the atmosphere, maybe it was the way we were looked after.

By the time we left, what stayed with me wasn’t just the visit but the quiet feeling of belonging the place managed to create.

text.text:
We had heard good things about Moonrock Bar, and while the drinks themselves were good, the overall experience didn’t quite live up to other bars we’ve visited in Tainan.

The atmosphere felt a bit rigid and lacking warmth, technically fine, but missing that sense of soul or ease that makes you want to stay longer.

Booking was also frustrating. We called to reserve for a group of 8 on a Monday night and were told the maximum was 6, and that separate tables weren’t possible. However, when we called back as two separate groups, we were suddenly able to secure two tables, despite being told earlier there was no space.

It left us feeling a bit put off. Decent drinks, but the experience and flexibility didn’t match the quality of other bars in the city.

Our favourite drinks were:
Skillability ver. 4
Moondust
Mehita

text.text:
I would give Moonrock a solid four-star rating. The bar is clearly inventive, especially when it comes to its cocktails. I ordered four drinks, and while each one was creative and had a fun, gimmicky flair, they didn’t quite hit the mark for me in terms of flavor. I tend to prefer drinks that prioritize taste over concept. That said, it’s easy to see why the bar has received numerous awards, and I can understand why others might find the experience exciting and memorable.
The food, however, was a pleasant surprise. It was well above average and genuinely excellent. If the drinks had matched the quality and deliciousness of the food, this would have easily been a five-star experience.

text.text:
09 July 2024

One of the restaurants that must visit while in Tainan.
Specialize in milkfish.

Pork rice is just normal taste. Their braised tofu and egg are good.
And they have garlic rice that I forgot to take a picture of it, it is very popular in this restaurant. But I prefer the white rice.
But then, I think you should give it a try while you're here.

The star here is the milkfish. I love it both ways, soup or fry, superb.

Arriving at 8.30 or 9.00 in the morning, hoping could try their fried fish skin or fried fish maw. But I had forgotten which one is that, sold out.
I think you should come really early, like be the first customer to try that 😂

Service is surprisingly good. I mean it's a small restaurant, how good can it be.
But the lady boss was very nice, take time to take our order cause my chinese is pretty bad, english communication is a bit hard.

Will be back if happens to be in Tainan

text.text:
This Japanese-French dessert restaurant features seasonal set menus in a bright, refined space with cozy seating. The owner was exceptionally kind and detailed in describing each creation and guiding the tasting process.

I chose a Seasonal Set with tea pairing and also tried a Seasonal Parfait. Every item was artistic and imaginative—each one looked like a miniature masterpiece. The flavors in the set were balanced, not overly sugary, with just enough savory notes to keep the experience engaging. The Parfait was particularly impressive: visually stunning and layered with up to 15 distinct components, offering a variety of textures and tastes. The total cost came to about 1,500 NTD, plus the service fee.

For those who appreciate desserts that are both inventive and visually striking, this is a must-visit. The fusion of Japanese delicacy and French elegance results in a dining experience that’s rich in detail and harmony. The sweetness is finely tuned, allowing you to indulge fully without it becoming overwhelming—comparable to what you might find in a Michelin-level patisserie.

text.text:
【Tainan Food: Katsura Dessert】

Katsura Dessert, located in Tainan, is a dessert shop I've been eyeing for a while.

They offer "platter dessert sets," each with a different theme each season.

The desserts are presented with a French flair,

but their soul lies in the taste of Taiwan.

This season's theme is "Grocery Store."

From snacks and glass marbles to the sweet atmosphere of a grocery store,

these are cleverly transformed into a series of desserts.

The dishes incorporate many Taiwanese elements,

such as hawthorn, seaweed, brown rice, white sesame seeds, peanuts, and millet porridge,

recombined in a familiar yet fresh way.

The desserts are well-made, the work of a seasoned pastry chef; the crumbly, crispy, and light textures are all perfectly executed.

Plate desserts are inherently more subjective,

but I found the overall design interesting, and one or two dishes particularly caught my eye, making it worthwhile.

The tea pairing is refreshing and smooth, and pairs naturally with the desserts.

Both the desserts and drinks are reasonably priced.

Overall, it's an interesting experiment.

More and more chefs are willing to make desserts the main focus,

and explore more and more design possibilities.

It's definitely a boon for dessert lovers!

originalText.text:
【食在台南：楿 Katsura Dessert 】

位於台南的「楿」是我觀望許久的甜點店，
提供「盤式甜點套餐」，每一季都有不同主題。
甜點呈現方式帶有法式精神，
靈魂是屬於台灣的味道。

本季套餐主題是「柑仔店」。
從零嘴、玻璃彈珠到柑仔店的甜香氛圍，
巧妙轉化為一系列甜點。

料理中運用了許多台灣元素，
如山楂、海苔、玄米、白芝麻、花生、麵茶等，
以熟悉卻嶄新的方式重新組合。

甜點製作水準不錯，是成熟甜點師的作品，
該酥的酥、該脆的脆、該輕盈的也非常到位。
盤式甜點本來就較偏向主觀喜好，
但我覺得整套設計算是有趣，
其中一兩道讓我眼睛一亮，也就值得了。

Tea pairing 清爽順口，與甜點的搭配也自然，
甜點和飲品價格都很親切實惠。

整體來說是個有趣的嘗試，
越來越多主廚願意把甜點當成主角，
設計發想更多不同可能，
絕對是甜點愛好者的福音～

The restaurant was incredibly friendly! Remember to book in advance via Facebook 🫡 They said the date was full, but someone cancelled, so we got to eat there 🥹 No need to order large or small portions, they'll serve the food according to the number of people. Everything we ordered that day was delicious!

For two people, we ordered the following dishes, totaling $1600:

📍Oyster Vermicelli ($180): The seasoning was amazing, with sesame oil, shallots, garlic, and scallions—so fragrant and delicious 😭

📍Garlic Chicken ($600): The chicken was incredibly tender, I wonder if it was pressure-cooked, and full of collagen 🤤

📍Chicken Wings ($80): The grilling was superb; not a single part was burnt! 🤣

📍Grilled Ribs ($320): I'm a bit sensitive to pork smells, and I felt there was still a slight lingering odor, but a squeeze of lemon made it much better 😌. Grilled to perfection!

📍Grilled Fish ($400): Crispy skin and tender meat 🤩 Highly recommended! The owner must have used a grill rack to press it down while grilling; the moment you peeled it open, the hot air rushed out—so intense! 😍

So satisfying! Next time I want to order loofah and clams 😁

text.text:
The owner's superb grilling skills are absolutely amazing!

Without fancy plating or skewer decorations, the focus is on delivering absolutely delicious ingredients, saving us the expense of expensive fancy packaging. The fresh ingredients, skillfully prepared by the owner, showcase years of accumulated expertise, resulting in such a wonderful texture, aroma, and juicy sweetness. Reservations must be made through the official Line account. The owner will provide a menu in advance, and you can place your order a few days before your visit. This arrangement ensures a smooth dining experience. The owner's surprisingly fast service, keeping the restaurant full even while working alone, is truly impressive. The garlic chicken is a must-order, and the bone-in beef steak surpasses the flavors of many high-end restaurants. I personally don't like eel, but the owner's grilled eel was surprisingly chewy and delicious!

Even the fine bones were meticulously removed. It's definitely worth a trip to Tainan to experience the owner's culinary skills firsthand! Excellent value for money!

text.text:
Once you make a reservation, you have to order. The chef determines the portion size based on the number of people, but the price is based on the portion size, which makes the price opaque and adds a bit of excitement (?). This time, the average price per person was NT$770, and I left with NT$11 left after paying. It was quite a surprise 😂

The dishes are based on personal preference.

1. Garlic Chicken: A must-order. The chicken is incredibly tender, like what you'd find in a local chicken shop. The gravy at the bottom of the dish is incredibly flavorful when poured over rice, though it's quite salty.

2. Grilled Spare Ribs: A must-order. They're supposed to be pork, but they're so tender they resemble short ribs 🤯

3. Grilled Chicken Wings: Pretty good. They're so crispy you'd think they'd been deep-fried, but without the grease. The grilling is almost as good as air-frying.

~ Fortunately, I'll order more of the following if I'm interested~
4. Seasonal Fish: Two people ordered the morning fry, which was also crispy. The skin and even the fins were so crispy that you could eat them straight away, just like fried river shrimp. However, the juiciness on the underside wasn't particularly well-retained, and the meat was a bit dry and tough.
5. Sausage: Original sausage with fresh garlic slices. It wasn't bad, but I didn't particularly like it either.

I saw a lot of people at the tables next to us ordering grilled clams and oysters, and they were quite large. Perhaps we'll see when more people come.

text.text:
On my first visit, I ordered a few dishes, and they were all delicious. Here are a few reviews:

Grilled Cabbage: Initially, I was skeptical after reading other people's reviews praising it, but I was pleasantly surprised. The aroma of the grilled vegetables paired with the special sauce with a hint of garlic was amazing. Even after grilling, the vegetables retained their moisture. Highly recommended!

Grilled Rice Balls: Another dish that surprised me. The grilled rice balls I usually get outside are either too dry or lack flavor; these, however, are coated with mentaiko (spicy roe) and baked until crispy. The addition of egg yolk is the perfect finishing touch, making the overall combination harmonious – a must-order.

Stir-fried Udon Noodles: The sauce was absolutely perfect for me. A common problem with creamy sauces is that they become cloying as the noodles become less plentiful; but this sauce contained what I believe to be kelp and seaweed, adding a touch of sweetness and making it more mellow. Another must-order.

Crab Croquettes: The price is a bit high for the portion size, but you know it's worth it after just one bite. The outer layer is golden and crispy, topped with salmon roe and crab paste; the filling seems to be a blend of cream and crab paste, with a touch of crab meat. Overall, the sauce and the fried crust are perfectly balanced – a must-order.

Summary: Very high revisit rate.

第一次到訪，隨便點個幾樣口味都很好吃，下面挑幾樣評論：

烤高麗菜：
一開始看其他人評論說多好吃多好吃讓我感到懷疑，結果一吃驚為天人，烤蔬菜的香氣搭配特製醬汁帶點蒜味，即使是烤過也還保有蔬菜豐富的水分，推薦必點

烤飯糰：
也是讓我驚艷的一道。平常在外面吃到的烤飯糰不外乎都是偏乾柴或味道不足；這邊的的烤飯糰表層抹上明太子醬後烘烤，表面酥脆；再佐以蛋黃畫龍點睛，整體的搭配非常和諧，必點

炒烏龍麵：
醬汁實在是太對我胃口了, 一般奶油醬的通病是吃到後半段麵量量變少後容易膩口；但這道的醬汁加入了我猜是昆布海苔的成分，讓原本易膩口的奶油增添了一點甜味變得更溫潤，這也必點

蟹肉可樂餅：
價位以份量來說偏高，但入口後就知道是值這個價格的；外皮金黃酥脆，佐以些許鮭魚卵以及蟹膏；內餡應該是奶油再加上蟹膏調合，加上些許蟹肉；整體來說醬汁的調配與炸皮搭配非常和諧，必點

總結：
再訪率非常高

text.text:
The location of Lakeshore Hotel Tainan was extremely convenient. There were plenty of food spots and attractions within walking distance, and the hotel also provided free parking, which was very thoughtful.

The Tainan Art Museum Building 2 was right next to the hotel, and Bao’an Road, known for its many popular local eats, was also easily walkable. In a city center like Tainan where parking is hard to find, this location truly deserved full marks.

The room was not as spacious as expected, but it was clean and well maintained. The bed and pillows were very comfortable, and the bathroom had a separate wet and dry area with a bathtub, which was a nice bonus. Hot water came quickly, and the water pressure was strong.

Our booking did not include breakfast this time. Friends mentioned that the breakfast at Lakeshore Hotel Tainan was very rich, including freshly made beef soup, so I would love to try it next time.

Considering the price, the overall value was excellent. I would definitely stay here again on my next trip to Tainan.

text.text:
Beautiful decor, good service, and interesting food based on Chinese medicine — but the food was not delicious. My chicken soup was rather bland. English menu available.

I’m not rating from a medicinal perspective — just purely on deliciousness — and in that sense, I do not recommend coming here. It has received a distinction from the Michelin Guide as a Bib Gourmand but I need to disagree. Skip, unless you’re really into Traditional Chinese Medicine.

text.text:
Really unique and difficult to describe. The staff welcomes you recommending you not to eat certain soups if you're on your period.
The soup itself is a broth made of spices and oil, the taste is difficult to describe but it's delicate and a bit bitter. Soup comes with pork meat and a generous side of oyster mushrooms
The noodles portion is also generous.
My palate had troubles understanding what was I eating, but I enjoyed it.
The set comes with tea and a dessert. I chose red beans with tangerine.
It's worth for the experience, tho I'm not sure I'd eat this everyday.

ChIJx7BkVQB3bjQRggT-VmNzwFU.formatted_address: No. 2號, Lane 141, Section 1, Beimen Rd, West Central District, Tainan City, Taiwan 700

text.text:
I had the pleasure to discover this restaurant in Hualien one year ago. I loved the dishes so much that I had no other choice to go to the newly opened place in Tainan.
Without surprise, everything was excellent: the very kind service, the flavor of the dishes which are a delicate fusion between Japanese and Western cuisine (try the Japanese style hamburger with the demi-glace sauce or the chicken breast with different types of sauces) and the atmosphere of the place (cosy and friendly with vintage Japanese music) that makes you want to stay till the late evening.
The menu set, at a very reasonable price, allows you to try different side dishes that are all delicious as well.
Besides, if you come to Tainan by train, the restaurant is just near the train station, which is quite convenient.

text.text:
The burger patty was perfectly seared on the outside, and the meat inside was firm and juicy. I absolutely loved their scallion and salt sauce! The homemade scallion and salt sauce had a satisfying crunch of onions, a perfect balance with the savory burger patty 😋

Each set meal includes two homemade side dishes, all very refreshing 👍. The rice portion is adjustable; I recommend the "more rice" option (no extra charge) for those with a larger appetite, as the burger patty is so delicious and goes perfectly with rice 😆!

This time I also ordered the onion tamagoyaki (with cheese inside), which was perfectly cooked and tender. The onion topping added another layer of flavor, a perfect finishing touch ✨

I felt incredibly satisfied after eating~ I'll definitely come back next time 🥰

originalText.text:
漢堡排表皮煎得微焦，裡面的肉質紮實又多汁，真的好喜歡這家的蔥鹽醬口味！店家自製的蔥鹽醬吃得到洋蔥脆脆的口感，跟鹹香的漢堡排達到了完美平衡😋

每份套餐可以選兩樣自製小菜，口味都很清爽👍白飯份量可以調整，建議食量大的人選「飯多」（無需加價），因為漢堡排真的太美味、太下飯了😆！

這次還加點了洋蔥泥玉子燒（裡面還有起司），口感煎得很軟嫩，上面的洋蔥泥讓口味更有層次，很有畫龍點睛的效果✨

吃完覺得超級滿足～下次會再來🥰

text.text: The restaurant is tucked away in a small alley next to a bustling main road; after going down a small slope, you push open the door to enter a small courtyard. The interior has a minimalist Japanese style. I sat at the bar that day and ordered the #RichRedWineMushroomBurgerSteakSet, which included two side dishes of your choice. Having tried the burger steak at Manatsu, I was deeply impressed by its thick, substantial, and juicy texture. The perfect balance of lean and fat meat was so satisfying! It's currently my number one Japanese burger steak. 🫶🏻

originalText.text: 店子隱於熱鬧大路旁的小巷，下小坡後推門進小庭園。用餐室內是日系簡約風，當日坐吧台，點了#香醇紅酒蘑菇漢堡排套餐，可自選2個小菜。吃過真夏的漢堡排就被厚、實在、噴汁深深吸引，肥瘦肉適中，好滿足～暫是心中第一位的日式漢堡排🫶🏻

text.text:
首推抹茶拿鐵，現點現作的抹茶，再加入鮮奶中，好看又好拍；
可以用LinePay支付，很方便。

A slow-tempo style shop located in the rush street might catch your eyes when passing by. Out of tune with the busy traffic there, its lantern hanging below the eaves swings gracefully, seems to wave the hand and attract you in.

As a matcha lover, I’m always interested in looking for teashops serving matcha and giving it a try. The funny thing is, I just love the color more than the slightly bitter taste. Because I’m also a, amateur, photo-shooter chasing any beautiful sight and capturing it before it disappeared.

Matcha latte is one of the top sellers, and probably is a cup of time-consuming tea, since it’s a make-to-order item and needs to wait for awhile. I think it makes sense that tasting matcha need a peaceful mood and you would calm down in such vibe after minutes. Looking the interior surrounding, it’s definitely worthy of shooting for some beautiful sights.

ChIJDxFecYh2bjQRV3VgDV4K9YU.editorial_summary: Minimalist cafe specializing in a variety of matcha-based sweets, plus tea & free Wi-Fi.

text.text:
I recommend this shop to people who love matcha.

The shop was small but relaxing and comfortable. The design was simple and beautiful, with green colors and wooden tables. It also had a Japanese-style design, which made the atmosphere calm and relaxing.

I ordered a matcha mille crepe cake, a matcha Swiss roll, and a cup of matcha. The desserts looked very pretty. The mille crepe cake was soft and creamy, but a little bit bitter, and the Swiss roll was light and sweet. The matcha flavor was strong and delicious.

I really enjoyed the desserts and the atmosphere. Everything tasted fresh and nice.

Unfortunately, there were too many people that day, so there were no indoor seats available. If you want to visit, maybe you can make a reservation first.

text.text:
I felt uncomfortable with the silly rule of a minimum spend of 130 TWD per person, which cannot be shared across members of the same group. Because of this I couldn't order what I wanted.

If the minimum charge was shared across the group I would be more understanding, but the fact that you need to assign each item in your order to each member of your group to ensure you meet the minimum spend per person is ridiculous. This made ordering complicated and sharing desserts difficult.

The basque cake was good with a decently strong matcha flavour, and the crêpe cake was only okay.

text.text:
*No AC Alert (but u probably won't need it)

More than a half-decade-old ice store! We only ordered lemon aiyu jelly topped with shaved ice. It's so vital amid scorching heat. And it surely did its job to cool us down and refresh our tastes. You can feel the bouncing, semi-liquid texture of the jelly in your mouth, yet it’s not mushy & hollow enough to dissolve right away, offering some realness and solidness. The shaved ice was such a cherry on top (or should I just say shaved ice on top as a new expression). You will beg for more because it's so compatible with the jelly. The ice added a breeze and an extra crunchiness to this dessert. Even in winter, don’t leave out the ice.

I went for the sandwich deal with an upgraded drink. The sandwich was massive. The drink was super fresh and well made. Everything was well made. It was a really good experience and I was grateful to have tried it.

text.text:
I enjoy visiting both historical and modern architecture, and before my recent trip to Tainan, I booked a visit to this mansion. It's currently being restored using modern techniques, resulting in a smoother walkway and more comfortable spaces. However, I felt the added wood color didn't blend well with the original stone. After consulting with AI (an online learning platform), I decided to explore this area: I recommend weathered wood colors, especially light gray or driftwood. This not only preserves the wood's warm texture but also allows its color to harmonize perfectly with the natural grain and historical feel of the granite, creating a more unified and timeless look.

Additionally, the Wufu Store, built around the same time, uses dark wood, which I also found quite attractive; the wood color combination is a bit of a shame.

Furthermore, the interior and walls display numerous architectural models and design information, but there are relatively few historical artifacts or other items, mainly concentrated in the registration area. This is a pity. A historical video or a more extensive presentation, similar to the Bopiliao Historic District, would have created a stronger connection to the past.

There are two restrooms inside with walls that have a historical feel. You need to bring your own toilet paper. Since the visit is free, you can buy postcards to show your support. The attached café was closed when I went on a Sunday, so there was less space to see. A guided tour might be more informative.

originalText.text:
本身喜歡參觀歷史跟現代建築，在近期決定去台南之前就預定參觀這個宅邸，目前是以現代工法修復歷史建築的方式，雖然修復後走起來平穩、空間舒適，但後來修復的木頭色覺得與原本的石材沒有融和感，詢問AI後給出的建議，我覺得也是一個方向：
推薦風化木色，特別是淺灰木色或漂流木色。這樣不僅能保留木材的溫潤質感，更能讓其色彩與硓𥑮石的自然肌理和歷史滄桑感達成高度的和諧與融合，使整體建築呈現出更統一且更具時間感的樣貌。
另外同時期的五福商店則是用深木色個人也覺得滿好看的，木材顏色配搭略微可惜。

此外，建築內部及牆面擺設了不少的建築模型及建築設計介紹，歷史關聯或舊物的部分較少，主要集中在報到處那個空間，覺得蠻可惜的，如果可以拍個歷史影片或是可能像剝皮寮歷史街區一樣有些較多篇幅的介紹，會與過去比較有連結感。

裡面有兩間洗手間牆面也蠻有歷史斑駁感的，要自備衛生紙，為免費參觀，可以買明信片支持，那天週日去附設的咖啡廳沒開，所以可以看的空間又有少一些，團體導覽或許比較有內容可以聽。

A fantastic visit! Guided tours are available by appointment.

Professional and insightful guided tours are available.

Dining spaces where you can rest and eat.

Creations by an artist from Myanmar.

Old-fashioned charm is a unique gift of Tainan. The old exudes a serene and refined atmosphere, a timeless elegance.

The Huang Residence on Xinyi Street in Tainan is a century-old, elegant mansion. Many details reveal the original owner's taste and the building's refined taste. For example, the wooden door is inscribed with the characters "进" (Jin) and "悦" (Yue), the names of the former owners (first photo). This display of ownership isn't uncommon, but in this meticulously detailed mansion, it adds a touch of understated yet tranquil romance. 💕

The second floor features an exhibition of paintings by Burmese artist Chung Yi-hua. He has lived in Tainan for many years, and his works often depict corners of Tainan's alleyways using pointillism. The overall style blends perfectly with the old house; the curator's selection of paintings is excellent, seamlessly integrating them into the space! The artist himself will also be there to chat with you.

The side room offers meals from Senjian Dining Hall and Xu Laifu (it's so nice to have food 😋). With both mental and physical nourishment satisfied, how could you not love Tainan? 🥰

台南信義街硓𥑮石黃宅，一座百年優雅宅第，從許多細節可以看出原房主的品味與建築的雅緻之處。像是木門中間刻著「進」、「悅」兩字，是以前黃宅男女主人的名字(照片第1張)，這樣的主權地位展現方式並非首見，但放在這充滿細節的大宅子裡，特別顯出那既高調又靜謐的浪漫啊💕。

二樓空間有緬甸籍藝術家鐘奕華的畫作展，他落角台南多年，作品多以點描法描繪台南巷弄ㄧ隅，整體風格跟老屋好搭呀，策展人真會選畫，無違和融入！畫家本人也會在現場跟你聊天喔。

側間屋廳裡有供應森間食堂和許來福的餐點（有吃食真好😋)，精神與肉體糧食ㄧ次餵飽，你怎麼可能不愛府城🥰

text.text:
This Huang House, built of cooing stone on Xinyi Street in Tainan, is more than just an old house; it embodies the everyday lives and architectural craftsmanship of early Taiwanese history. Its history dates back to the Qing Dynasty, when Tainan Prefecture was Taiwan's political and economic center, thriving commercial activity. As a street-style building combining a shopfront and a residence, the Huang House perfectly reflects the prefectural capital's "front shop, back home" business model.

The Huang House's unique cooing stone building material is closely tied to Taiwan's geography and historical background. Coroing stone is actually coral reef rock. In times of inconvenient transportation and scarce building materials, especially in coastal settlements like Tainan, the ancient inhabitants utilized local resources, using these hard and durable marine rocks to build walls. This not only demonstrates the ingenuity of early Taiwanese residents in overcoming environmental limitations, but also bears witness to Tainan's deep connection to the ocean.

Through modern urban planning during the Japanese colonial period, Tainan's ancient cooing stone house remained intact. It silently bears the historical imprints of different eras, from the commercial prosperity of the Qing Dynasty to the changes in life during the Japanese colonial period, all told through its simple walls.

Thus, the Huang House on Coral Stone is not only the Huang family's residence, but also a valuable asset that carries Taiwan's history, local culture, and architectural wisdom. It reminds us that before the advent of modern concrete skyscrapers, Taiwan's history was solidly built, piece by piece, using locally sourced stones.

originalText.text:
這座位於台南信義街的咾咕石黃宅，不只是一棟古厝，更是台灣早期歷史中，常民生活與建築工藝的具體縮影。它的歷史可追溯至清朝，當時的台南府城是台灣的政治經濟中心，商業活動興盛。黃宅作為一棟結合店面與住家的街屋，正好反映了當時府城「前店後家」的商業模式。

而黃宅最獨特的咾咕石建材，則與台灣的地理環境和歷史背景緊密相關。咾咕石其實就是珊瑚礁岩，在交通不便、建材匱乏的時代，特別是像台南這樣的沿海聚落，先民們就地取材，利用這些堅硬耐用的海中石塊來築牆。這不僅展現了台灣早期居民克服環境限制的生活智慧，也見證了台南與海洋地景的深厚淵源。

進入日治時期，台南雖然經歷了現代化的都市規劃，但這座古老的咾咕石黃宅仍被保留下來。它默默地承載著不同時代的歷史印記，從清代的商業繁華到日治時期的生活變遷，都透過其質樸的牆面娓娓道來。

因此，咾咕石黃宅不僅是黃家的宅邸，更是承載著台灣歷史、在地文化與建築智慧的重要資產。它提醒著我們，在那些現代化的水泥高樓出現之前，台灣的歷史是透過一塊塊就地取材的石頭，被紮實地砌築起來的。

text.text:
It was a privilege to visit Chihkan Tower (also known as Fort Provintia), one of the oldest and most historically significant sites in Tainan’s West Central District. Originally constructed by the Dutch in 1653 as an administrative and commercial centre, the complex has since evolved into a beautifully preserved heritage site featuring tranquil gardens, koi ponds, and intricate stone carvings.

The visit offered a valuable opportunity to revisit and better understand Taiwan’s layered colonial history. Interpretive displays trace the Dutch period (1624–1662), followed by Koxinga (Zheng Chenggong)’s 1662 victory and the establishment of the Kingdom of Tungning, and later the Qing dynasty era — including the notable imperial stone steles commissioned by Emperor Qianlong.

What makes the site particularly compelling is the visible cultural fusion: the original Western fortification foundations now coexist with later Chinese-style temples and pavilions. This architectural and historical layering highlights Tainan’s role as Taiwan’s earliest capital and a crossroads of political, cultural, and maritime exchange.

originalText.text:
It was a privilege to visit Chihkan Tower (also known as Fort Provintia), one of the oldest and most historically significant sites in Tainan’s West Central District. Originally constructed by the Dutch in 1653 as an administrative and commercial centre, the complex has since evolved into a beautifully preserved heritage site featuring tranquil gardens, koi ponds, and intricate stone carvings.

The visit offered a valuable opportunity to revisit and better understand Taiwan’s layered colonial history. Interpretive displays trace the Dutch period (1624–1662), followed by Koxinga (Zheng Chenggong)’s 1662 victory and the establishment of the Kingdom of Tungning, and later the Qing dynasty era — including the notable imperial stone steles commissioned by Emperor Qianlong.

What makes the site particularly compelling is the visible cultural fusion: the original Western fortification foundations now coexist with later Chinese-style temples and pavilions. This architectural and historical layering highlights Tainan’s role as Taiwan’s earliest capital and a crossroads of political, cultural, and maritime exchange.

Chihkan Tower is not only historically important but also serene and atmospheric — a must-visit landmark for anyone wishing to appreciate the origins and cultural evolution of Taiwan.

ChIJ7Ruxw3x2bjQRZk4MEAECIq4.editorial_summary: Striking space for contemporary Taiwanese art & sculpture exhibitions, with a restaurant & cafe.

text.text:
An art museum situated in the middle of Tainan. From what I understand, they don’t have a constant exhibit, instead of that, their exhibitions are time-based and always-changing. So I recommend that you check what exhibitions are being displayed that day.

The tickets are 200 TWD for adults and 100 TWD for students, which is very reasonable. Although not necessary, it might be a good idea to buy your tickets in advance to avoid the potential of a queue.

Although my knowledge of art is limited, I think this gallery is more focused on contemporary art, rather than classical art. The gallery is divided into 3 floors, each one displaying a different collection. Every gallery is equipped with excellent explanations written by the curators in both Mandarin and English. Altogether, it should probably take you 1 hr and a bit to finish this museum.

In short, I recommend this art museum. 10/10

text.text:
A Timeless Journey Back to the 1930s!

Visiting Hayashi Department Store is like stepping into a time machine. The highlight for me was the iconic elevator with the floor indicator dial—it’s one of the oldest in Taiwan and still maintains its original mechanical charm. I also highly recommend heading to the rooftop to see the Suehiro Shrine, the only rooftop shrine in a store in Taiwan.

The interior is filled with beautifully preserved vintage furniture, antique radios, and retro TVs, creating a nostalgic atmosphere that is perfect for photos. It’s not just a shopping mall; it’s a living museum of Tainan’s history. A must-visit landmark!

text.text: This historical building was once not only the highest architecture but also the one and only shopping mall in Tainan back in the 30’s, under the Japanese occupancy. Nowadays it is still one of the most interesting retail and tourist attractions in this fascinating city, offers customers many selections of snacks, gifts, fashion, crafts and arts. Imagine you’re shopping in a ambience shop that’s surrounded by a eclectic vibe, as well as a colourful merchandise collection. A must see spot and hot spot when you come to Tainan.
